description:
An environmental site assessment team needs to determine which chemicals are detectable by a Photoionization Detector (PID) with a 10.6 eV lamp using CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/). For Benzene, Methane, Carbon tetrachloride, Trichloroethylene, Chloroform, and Styrene, identify the CAS Number and Ionization Energy. A chemical is DETECTABLE if its Ionization Energy is <= 10.6 eV. Create a report at /home/ga/Documents/pid_assessment.txt using the following format for each:

Chemical: [Name]
CAS: [CAS Number]
Ionization Energy: [Value] eV
PID Detectable: [DETECTABLE or NOT DETECTABLE]

Include a SUMMARY section at the end of the file counting the total detectable and not detectable chemicals.
